title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Repository Trace: Local Server Gates
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git local server gates increment in a bounded Git automation and trust policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Local Server Gates
steps.instruction:
theory case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Local Server Gates contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate local convenience hooks, ci checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

steps.title: Bound Local Server Gates
steps.instruction:
theory case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Local Server Gates application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate local convenience hooks, ci checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.

content.text: Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. A defensible solution applies Local Server Gates, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 2
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A pre-commit hook prevents all invalid commits from reaching a shared repository. This fails the Local Server Gates contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
